title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Automation and Data Client Systems · Guided Program Studio
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for pagination, filtering, links, rate limits, versioning, and resumable work, producing resumable rate-aware API traversal. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers resumable rate-aware API traversal while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Encode repeated parameters, arrays, dates, sorting, and filters according to the API contract without manual concatenation. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Coordinate server guidance, local quotas, concurrency, backoff, and caller priorities without synchronized bursts. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pagination Models
steps.instruction:
workshop case — civic-data staging importer follows cursor c3, repeated link c3, and rate limit 30/minute while records change between pages. Encode filters, detect loops, checkpoint stable identity, resume after failure, and avoid duplicates or gaps. Competency probe: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
